title: sphaeralcea incana var. cuneata
intro: Sphaeralcea incana var. cuneata, also known as the Grayish Globemallow or Gray Globe Mallow, is a variety of flowering plant in the mallow family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to be effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Sphaeralcea incana var. cuneata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of colds and coughs. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, and it can also be made into a salve or ointment by mixing the plant material with a carrier oil.
